summary: Matched most of melee/lb/lb_00B0.c, bringing the file report to 95.36% for 7.64 KB. The main code changes replace NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs for lb_8000C490 and lb_8000C868 with interpolation logic for HSD_JObj/HSD_Joint transforms, including translation and scale blending, Euler-to-quaternion conversion, quaternion sign correction, and HSD_QuatLib_8037EF28 interpolation. The header was cleaned up by removing static/internal declarations from lb_00B0.h, adding address comments to exported declarations, and switching several f32 declarations to float. scratches.txt entries for the affected functions were updated from percentage statuses to OK.
what_changed: Implemented lb_8000C490, replacing a NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub with weighted blending of two HSD_JObj transforms into a destination HSD_JObj.
Implemented lb_8000C868, replacing a NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub with weighted blending of an HSD_Joint bind/default transform and an HSD_JObj transform into a destination HSD_JObj.
Added use of <math.h> and <baselib/quatlib.h> in lb_00B0.c to support ABS checks, EulerToQuat, Quaternion, and HSD_QuatLib_8037EF28 usage.
Moved static helper prototypes out of lb_00B0.h and into lb_00B0.c for internal functions such as lb_8000B9D8, lbFindJObjWithAObj, lb_8000BECC, lbGetFreeColorRegImpl, and lbGetFreeAlphaRegImpl.
Reformatted lb_00B0.h exported prototypes with address comments such as /* 00C490 */ and /* 00C868 */ and used float in declarations.
Updated config/GALE01/scratches.txt statuses for func_8000C228 through func_8000CE30 from percentage values to OK, including func_8000C490 and func_8000C868.
smart_moves: The transform interpolation code handles both Euler and quaternion rotation representations by checking HSD_JObj flags for 0x20000 before deciding whether to reinterpret rotate as a Quaternion or convert Euler angles with EulerToQuat.

Both new interpolation paths include a fast/simple path for nearly identical Euler rotations: lb_8000C490 uses an ABS threshold of 0.01f per axis, while lb_8000C868 uses a threshold of 1 per axis, then copies Euler rotation and clears the quaternion flag.
Before quaternion interpolation, the code compares squared distance of quat1 - quat2 versus quat1 + quat2 and negates the second quaternion when needed, avoiding interpolation along the long arc.
The destination JObj flags are updated consistently after rotation output: quaternion output sets 0x20000 and dirty/changed flag 0x40, while direct Euler copy clears 0x20000 and sets 0x40.
Internal static declarations were removed from the public header and kept in the C file, reducing header pollution while preserving compile visibility for local helpers.
decomp_lessons: For lb transform-blend functions, matching may require preserving the exact rotation representation behavior: detect JOBJ quaternion flags, keep Euler direct-copy shortcuts, and only call quaternion interpolation when necessary.

When matching quaternion blending, compare squared sums and squared diffs to decide whether to negate the second quaternion before interpolation; this is a reusable pattern for shortest-path slerp-like behavior.
Public headers should not expose static helper declarations. Keep file-local helpers in the .c file and only export externally visible functions in the .h.
Address comments on header prototypes are useful for searchability and for aligning functions with the decomp report and scratches.
A high file-level match can still have large imperfect functions; in this PR lb_8000C868 remained the lowest-scoring function in the report at 62.88%, while lb_8000C490 was 92.41%.
naming_conventions: Known exported lb functions use the lb_8000XXXX address-based naming convention when no semantic name is established.
Semantic names are retained where already known, such as lbFindJObjWithAObj, lbGetFreeColorRegister, lbGetFreeAlphaRegister, lbDObjReqAnimAll, and lbDObjSetRateAll.
Function declarations in lb_00B0.h were annotated with short address comments in the form /* 00C490 */.

Local variable names in the matched code include descriptive quaternion/interpolation helpers in lb_8000C490, such as is_quat_1, is_quat_2, quat1, quat2, sum_square_diffs, and sum_square_sums; lb_8000C868 still uses many temp_f* names, indicating weaker semantic recovery there.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use explicit temporary scalar variables for quaternion component differences and sums before computing squared totals; this can help match compiler ordering.
Preserve conditional structure for Euler shortcut checks, including nested absolute-value checks in lb_8000C868 rather than collapsing into a single compound expression.
Use HSD_JObjGetFlags, HSD_JObjClearFlags, and HSD_JObjSetFlags rather than direct flag manipulation for JObj rotation-state side effects.
Cast rotate storage to Quaternion where the HSD_JObj rotate field is reused as quaternion data, as seen with assignments to Quaternion variables and casts around sp94 in lb_8000C868.
Weighted interpolation operands appear as source * arg8 plus source * arg9 for translate and scale fields; preserve operand order when trying to match.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that improve lb_8000C868 beyond 62.88% or rename it semantically.
Search for other HSD_QuatLib_8037EF28 callers to confirm the quaternion sign-correction pattern.
Look for follow-up header cleanup PRs that remove static declarations from public headers.
Check whether JObj flag 0x20000 receives a named constant in later baselib headers.
